commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the lint blocker, but maintainers still need to review the secrets-forwarding suppression before merge.; Needs attention: The hardening direction is security-positive, but the new secrets-inherit suppression needs maintainer security review before merge.; Review finding: [P2] Use a lint-compliant workflow file ordering — scripts/check-workflows.mjs:31 (sha=7ecf0c4aece1b32c9c124dc8872516134a30fbfc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hoists the Windows Testbox target ref into step env, adds a scoped zizmor suppression for one reusable workflow secrets handoff, broadens Workflow Sanity and `check:workflows` to audit all workflow YAML with zizmor, and updates CI docs/tests.

PR surface: Tests +17, Docs +1, Config +70, Other +36. Total +124 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and supplied proof: current main still interpolates `inputs.target_ref` directly inside the Windows probe run block, and the PR body includes before/after terminal output for `pnpm check:workflows`. I did not execute the workflow check locally because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the lint blocker, but maintainers still need to review the secrets-forwarding suppression before merge.

target.title: fix(ci): guard workflow template injection
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: #68428 tracks `zizmor` template-injection findings in GitHub workflow shell blocks; latest `main` had the originally listed workflows fixed, but an all-workflow scan still found the same class in `windows-testbox-probe.yml`.
- Why it matters: workflow_dispatch inputs can be attacker-controlled enough for `zizmor` to flag direct shell interpolation, and only checking changed workflows can miss stale findings in untouched workflow files.
- What changed: moved the Windows probe `target_ref` expression into step `env`, added a reasoned `secrets-inherit` suppression for the maintainer-only Update Migration package-acceptance lane, and made `check:workflows` / Workflow Sanity run the pinned `zizmor` hook across all workflow YAML files.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no product runtime behavior, release package behavior, or workflow permissions were expanded.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #68428
- Related #66884
- Related #68431
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `zizmor` template-injection findings in GitHub Actions workflows are removed and guarded against regression across all workflow YAML files.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `fix/ci-zizmor-template-injection`, Node 24.15.0 via nvm, pnpm 10.33.2, pre-commit 4.2.0, pinned repo `zizmor` hook v1.22.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm check:workflows`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/check-workflows.mjs CHANGELOG.md docs/ci.md`; `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/ci.md`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal output: `Lint GitHub Actions workflow files.......................................Passed`; `zizmor...................................................................Passed`; `No direct inputs interpolation found in composite run blocks.`; `All matched files use the correct format.`; `Docs MDX check passed (1 files, 388ms).`
- Observed result after fix: the all-workflow `zizmor` scan passed, including the previously affected release workflows and the remaining Windows Testbox probe finding.
- What was not tested: live GitHub Actions execution of Workflow Sanity on GitHub-hosted runners before PR creation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the patch, `pnpm check:workflows` failed with `error[template-injection]` at `.github/workflows/windows-testbox-probe.yml:69` and reported `zizmor` exit code 14.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: the Windows probe printed `${{ inputs.target_ref || github.ref }}` directly inside a PowerShell `run:` block, and Workflow Sanity only ran `actionlint` plus local guards rather than scanning every workflow with `zizmor`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: CI only ran `zizmor` on changed workflow files in `security-scm-fast`, so a stale finding in an untouched workflow could remain after the original four workflow files were fixed.
- Contributing context (if known): #66884 and #68431 fixed the workflows listed in #68428, but the issue stayed open and a full scan exposed one same-class finding elsewhere.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `pnpm check:workflows`, `.github/workflows/workflow-sanity.yml`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: every workflow YAML file passes `actionlint`, pinned `zizmor`, the composite input interpolation guard, and the conflict-marker guard.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is workflow YAML security linting, so the repo workflow wrapper and Workflow Sanity job directly exercise the affected surface.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `security-scm-fast` covers changed workflow files; this PR extends coverage to all workflow files.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None for product users. Contributors and maintainers get broader workflow security validation through `pnpm check:workflows` and Workflow Sanity.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[workflow changed] -> [security-scm-fast zizmor changed files]
[all workflows] -> [Workflow Sanity actionlint only]

After:
[workflow changed] -> [security-scm-fast zizmor changed files]
[all workflows] -> [Workflow Sanity/check:workflows actionlint + all-workflow zizmor + local guards]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the PowerShell run block no longer receives direct GitHub expression interpolation for `target_ref`; the value is passed through step `env` and read as `$env:TARGET_REF`.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local checkout, Node 24.15.0, pnpm 10.33.2, pre-commit 4.2.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): GitHub Actions workflows
- Relevant config (redacted): `.pre-commit-config.yaml` pinned `zizmor` hook v1.22.0 with `.github/zizmor.yml`

### Steps

1. Run `pnpm check:workflows`.
2. Run `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/check-workflows.mjs CHANGELOG.md docs/ci.md`.
3. Run `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/ci.md`.

### Expected

- `actionlint` passes.
- `zizmor` passes across all workflow YAML files.
- Local composite-action interpolation and conflict-marker guards pass.
- Formatting and docs MDX checks pass.

### Actual

- All expected checks passed locally.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: reproduced the all-workflow `zizmor` failure, fixed the Windows Testbox PowerShell interpolation, reran `pnpm check:workflows`, formatter, and docs MDX check.
- Edge cases checked: existing package-acceptance `secrets: inherit` medium finding is intentionally documented with a narrow `zizmor` suppression rather than silently weakening the scan severity.
- What you did **not** verify: live Workflow Sanity execution in GitHub Actions before opening this PR.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Workflow Sanity becomes slightly slower because it installs pre-commit and runs `zizmor` over all workflows.
  - Mitigation: this runs in the existing workflow-sanity lane and uses the pinned pre-commit hook already used by `security-scm-fast`.
- Risk: the `secrets-inherit` suppression could hide a future package-acceptance secret drift.
  - Mitigation: the suppression is scoped to the maintainer-dispatched Update Migration reusable workflow call and includes the reason inline.
target.author: wt-wsl
